LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT COLTON CALLAHAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS ELLIOT TAYLOR, RON TAYLOR AND 2441-2449 2ND STREET LLC'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS STRICKEN PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 435(B) BECAUSE IT IS NOT SUPPORTED BY ANY ALLEGATION AS TO A CONTRACT OR STATUTE AUTHORIZING SUCH FEES. (CODE CIV. PROC. 1033.5.) PLAINTIFF HAS 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IF HE CAN ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH A CONTRACTUAL OR STATUTORY BASIS SUPPORTING RECOVERY OF ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS SUPPORTED BY THE FRAUD ALLEGATIONS AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE THIS PORTION OF THE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600 EMAIL MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULES 2.550 AND 2.551. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE PORTIONS OF THE RECORDS TO BE SEALED; THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THOSE RECORDS; A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED; AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Seal The Unredacted Declaration Of Christopher Wimmer Including Without Limitation Exhibit E, F, I And J To The Unredacted Declaration Of Christopher Wimmer (Two Copies)
Off Calendar Nov-12-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANTS ELLIOT TAYLOR, RON TAYLOR, ET ASSETS, LLC, 2441-2449 2ND STREET LLC, AND TERRA NOVA INDUSTRIES' Motion To Seal The Unredacted Declaration Of Christopher Wimmer Including Without Limitation Exhibit E, F, I And J To The Unredacted Declaration Of Christopher Wimmer (Two Copies).

Grant. No opposition filed and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Jul-24-2020 Set For Trial On Apr-26-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Answer To Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-15-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. RICHARD J. IDELL, Esq. of Dickenson, Peatman & Fogarty (Tel: 707.261.7000) for Plaintiffs and Cross-defendants Elliott Taylor, Ron Taylor and 2441-2449 2nd Street, LLC and Cross-defendants Terra Nova Industries, Inc. and ET Assets, LLC;
Atty. CHRISTOPHER WIMMER, Esq. (Tel: 415-683-8867) for Defendants and Cross-Complainants
Colton Callahan and Michael Callahan.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS ELLIOT TAYLOR, RON TAYLOR, 2441-2449 2ND STREET LLC, ET ASSETS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY's Motion To Strike Answer To Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Taylor's motion to strike is denied. "Under the sham pleading doctrine, plaintiffs are precluded from amending complaints to omit harmful allegations, without explanation, from previous complaints to avoid attacks raised in demurrers or motions for summary judgment." (Deveny v. Entropin, Inc. (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 408, 425.) "The sham pleading doctrine is not 'intended to prevent honest complainants from correcting erroneous allegations ... or to prevent correction of ambiguous facts.' Instead, it is intended to enable courts 'to prevent an abuse of process.'" (Id. at 426.) In this case, defendants have explained the ostensible contradiction between the answer to the second amended complaint and the answer to the third amended complaint. Defendants are not attempting to omit harmful facts to dupe the court or parties. Rather, defendants' present contention is that Taylor is not currently an owner of Greener Future, LLC because he dissociated himself. Subsequent proceedings will resolve that contested factual issue.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Motion To Seal Third Amended Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-05-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: Plaintiffs/cross defendants having withdrawn their initial objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS COLTON CALLAHAN, MICHAEL CALLAHAN's Motion To Seal Third Amended Cross Complaint as follows:

Cross-Complainants' motion to seal portions of the Third Amended Cross-Complaint is granted. The motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551, and is supported by a sufficient evidentiary showing. (See Callahan Decl. 2-4.) The court sees no reason why it should depart from its prior ruling of November 2, 2019. Confidential business information is the proper subject of a motion to seal. (See Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1286.) The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

The Court will sign the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Continued To Jan-14-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS ELLIOT TAYLOR, RON TAYLOR, AND 2441-2449 2ND STREET LLC's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Cross-defendants Elliot Taylor, Ron Taylor and 2441-2449 2nd Street LLC's demurrer to the first amended cross-complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Cross-complainant has leave to add Michael Callahan, Greener Future, LLC, NAPA PAL, and ST5 Holdings, LLC as indispensable parties and to plead derivative claims on behalf of those entities. "Where the plaintiff seeks some type of affirmative relief which, if granted, would injure or affect the interest of a third party not joined, that party is in indispensable party." (Sierra Club, Inc. v. California Coastal Comm'n (1979) 95 Cal.App.3d 495, 501; see Olszewski v. Scriopps Health (2003) 30 Cal.4th 798, 8908 [person is indispensable party "when the judgment to be rendered necessarily must affect his rights"]; see also Deltakeeper v. Oakdale Irrig. Dist. (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 1092, 1106 [ordinarily, all parties to a conrtact are necessary parties in an action involving rights under the contract].) The court rejects movant's remaining arguments. The amended cross-complaint does not disclose on its face that the MOU was terminated. That is a factual issue. Even if the statute of frauds applies, it does not bar the claims in their entirety. The fraud allegations contain sufficient specificity.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Weill, Esq., Weil & Mazer, 415-421-0730, appearing for Plaintiff;
Peter Roldan, Esq., Emergent Law, 415-894-9284, appearing for Defendant Callahan;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT COLTON CALLAHAN MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendant Colton Callahan's motion to strike portions of plaintiffs Elliot Taylor, Ron Taylor and 2441-2449 2nd Street LLC's second amended complaint is granted in part and denied in part. The prayer for attorney's fees is stricken pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 435(b) because it is not supported by any allegation as to a contract or statute authorizing such fees. (Code Civ. Proc. 1033.5.) Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend if he can allege in good faith a contractual or statutory basis supporting recovery of attorney's fees. The prayer for punitive damages is supported by the fraud allegations and the motion to strike this portion of the complaint is denied.

Order is signed.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Weill, Esq., Weil & Mazer, 415-421-0730, appearing for Plaintiff;
Peter Roldan, Esq., emergent Law, 415-894-9284, , appearing for Defendant Callahan

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT COLTON CALLAHAN DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendant Colton Callahan's demurrer to Plaintiffs Elliot Taylor, Ron Taylor and 2441-2449 2nd Street LLC's second amended complaint is overruled in its entirety. There are no facts pled in the second amended complaint that are inconsistent with the unverified original complaint. Plaintiffs selectively quote from the original complaint, which alleged that "since Defendants refused to provide any of their required financing the Plainiffs decided to fund the project entirely by themselves." (Compl. 11 [emphasis added].) The added allegations in the second amended complaint regarding an oral contract are not inconsistent with the allegations in the original complaint. (See also id. 6, 7, 8, 9(d).) "Critically, the sham pleading doctrine 'cannot be mechanically applied.'" (JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. v. Ward (Mar. 28, 2019) 2019 WL 1396740 at *8. In particular, the doctrine "was not intended to preclude plaintiffs from providing additional and noncontradictory allegations." (Leasequip, Inc. v. Dapeer (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 394, 405 n.6; see also Zakk v. Diesel (Mar. 25, 2019) 2019 WL 1349530 at *11-12 [allegations regarding contract claims in third amended complaint "merely clarifies the basis for [plaintiff's] claims for relief" and sham pleading doctrine therefore did not apply].)

Likewise, the fraud allegations in the second amended complaint are not inconsistent with the allegation in the original complaint that both Michel Colton Callahan and Colton Callahan misrepresented their ability to fund the project and that they were each other's agents. (Compl. 5,6.) Nor has Plaintiff failed to plead the alleged misrepresentations about the Callahans' ability to finance the project with particularity. (SAC 8.) Finally, Plaintiff has pled that he is a co-manager of ST6 Holdings, LLC and therefore has standing pursuant to Corp. Code 17707.03(a) to bring an action for its dissolution.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600 email maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion To Quash Summons And Amended Complaint; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT COLTON CALLAHAN, MICHAEL CALLAHAN'S Motion To Quash Summons And Amended Complaint.

Defendants Michael Callahan and Colton Callahan's motion to quash service of summons is granted as to Michael Callahan and denied as to Colton Callahan. Michael Callahan has not made a general appearance and thus may still contend that he is not amenable to personal jurisdiction in California for this case. Plaintiffs Elliot Taylor and Ron Taylor have not shown that Michael Callahan has sufficient contacts with California to warrant the exercise of personal jurisdiction over him for this case either based on general or specific jurisdiction. The Taylors have shown that they validly served Colton Callahan with the summons and complaint. The Taylors have also shown that Colton Callahan has sufficient contacts with California to warrant the exercise of personal jurisdiction over him for this case based on specific jurisdiction.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
